main_bridge: The main missing bridge is a reusable characterization of joint-union independence in terms of iterated componentwise conditional independence.

next_direction.guidance: Prioritize broad lemmas that rewrite independence of unions into staged independence of components, especially across the semigraphoid-to-graphoid boundary.
next_direction.label: union_to_component_characterizations
next_direction.rationale: Most visible high-value targets fit this pattern, and it is the most promising route to reusable bridges, monotonicity consequences, and sharper structural separation.

saturated_areas: Isolated nonemptiness equivalences detached from the main union-bridge program
theory_kind: Abstract conditional independence theory for semigraphoids and graphoids

theory_summary.current_picture: This currently looks like the very start of a set-based conditional independence theory for semigraphoids and graphoids, with no verified derived lemmas yet. The visible problem queue is already concentrated around one main theme: rewriting independence of a large union into structured independence statements for smaller pieces, then exploiting those bridges to derive monotonicity and collapse phenomena. The graphoid-only target indicates that the theory is likely to split into a semigraphoid core and a stronger characterization layer that genuinely uses graphoid strength.
missing_pieces: A canonical reusable bridge from independence of a union to componentwise conditional independences
Clear converses and boundary statements showing what fails without full graphoid strength
A small monotonicity library propagating independence through subset relations
recurring_patterns: Equivalences between independence of a union and staged independence of its parts
Repeated use of decomposition, weak union, contraction, and symmetry over set unions
Attempts to separate semigraphoid-provable facts from stronger graphoid-only characterizations
representative_results: No derived theorems are visible yet; the leading targets are union-splitting equivalences.
A general shrink-monotonicity lemma for shrinking both independent arguments is being targeted in semigraphoids.
A collapse criterion from independence against Set.univ to total independence is also in the queue.
